name: Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 'Long Short-Term Memory', 1997

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'LSTM Cell (Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997)'. LEFT half: an inline-SVG LSTM cell diagram showing the cell state line across the top, three gates (forget, input, output) drawn as sigmoid units, a tanh candidate, the pointwise multiply/add operators, and inputs 'x_t' and 'h_{t-1}' entering, 'h_t' and 'C_t' leaving. RIGHT half: the SIX gate equations stacked, each with correct subscripts/superscripts: 'f_t = sigma(W_f [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_f)', 'i_t = sigma(W_i [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_i)', 'g_t = tanh(W_g [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_g)', 'o_t = sigma(W_o [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_o)', 'C_t = f_t * C_{t-1} + i_t * g_t', 'h_t = o_t * tanh(C_t)'. The left and right halves share the same top and bottom edge. Footnote at the bottom. ALSO, below the cell diagram, draw the UNROLLED chain over three time steps (t-1, t, t+1) as three equal-width aligned cell blocks passing C and h between them.
requirements: All six equations render subscripts (t, t-1, f, i, g, o) correctly, not as plain inline text
Each equation is on its own line, fully visible, with no horizontal clipping or overflow
The left diagram and right equation column share the same top and bottom edge (aligned halves)
The cell-state line, three gates, tanh, and pointwise operators are all drawn and labeled without overlap
Inputs x_t and h_{t-1} and outputs h_t and C_t are all labeled and not clipped
No element overlaps any other element on the slide
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
The left and right halves are of comparable width with a clear gutter between them
Every text element is at least 12px
The unrolled three-timestep chain shows three equal-width aligned cell blocks with C/h arrows between them, no overlap

ss_html:
LSTM Cell (Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997)

ft = σ(Wf [ht-1, xt] + bf)
it = σ(Wi [ht-1, xt] + bi)
gt = tanh(Wg [ht-1, xt] + bg)
ot = σ(Wo [ht-1, xt] + bo)
Ct = ft ⊙ Ct-1 + it ⊙ gt
ht = ot ⊙ tanh(Ct)
Long Short-Term Memory networks solve the vanishing gradient problem through gated cell states that selectively retain and forget information across time steps.
rv_html:
LSTM Cell (Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997)

ft = σ(Wf [ht-1, xt] + bf)
it = σ(Wi [ht-1, xt] + bi)
gt = tanh(Wg [ht-1, xt] + bg)
ot = σ(Wo [ht-1, xt] + bo)
Ct = ft ⊙ Ct-1 + it ⊙ gt
ht = ot ⊙ tanh(Ct)
Long Short-Term Memory networks solve the vanishing gradient problem through gated cell states that selectively retain and forget information across time steps.
